name: Calories
description: Energy content. A kilocalorie (kcal) is the amount of energy needed to raise 1 kg of water by 1°C. Often referred to as 'Calories' on food labels.

name: Water
description: Water content of the food. High water content generally means lower calorie density.

name: Protein
description: The total protein content. Proteins are chains of amino acids essential for building and repairing tissues, enzymes, and hormones.

name: Total Fat
description: All fat combined — saturated, unsaturated, and trans fats. Fat is essential for cell membranes, hormone production, and fat-soluble vitamin absorption.

name: Carbohydrates
description: Total carbohydrates — the body's primary energy source. Includes sugars, starches, and dietary fibre.

name: Dietary Fibre
description: Indigestible plant material that feeds gut bacteria, regulates blood sugar, and supports bowel health. Also spelled 'Dietary Fiber'.

name: Total Sugars
description: All sugars present — both naturally occurring (e.g. fructose in fruit) and added. Distinct from complex carbohydrates.

name: Saturated Fat
description: Fats with no double bonds in their carbon chain. Found mainly in animal products. Associated with elevated LDL cholesterol at high intakes.

name: Monounsaturated Fat
description: Fats with one double bond. Found in olive oil, avocado, and nuts. Generally considered heart-healthy.

name: Polyunsaturated Fat
description: Fats with multiple double bonds. Includes Omega-3 and Omega-6 families. Essential for brain function and inflammation regulation.

name: Omega-3 Fatty Acids
description: A family of polyunsaturated fats (ALA, EPA, DHA) critical for brain health, heart function, and reducing inflammation. Mainly found in fatty fish, flaxseed, and walnuts.

name: Omega-6 Fatty Acids
description: A family of polyunsaturated fats (mainly Linoleic Acid) important for cell structure. Common in vegetable oils and nuts. Balance with Omega-3 is important.

name: Trans Fat
description: Artificially hydrogenated fats or small natural amounts in ruminant fat. Artificial trans fats are associated with cardiovascular disease.

name: Cholesterol
description: A sterol found only in animal products. The body produces its own; dietary cholesterol has moderate impact on blood cholesterol in most people.

name: Calcium
description: Essential for bone and teeth structure, muscle contraction, nerve signalling, and blood clotting. The most abundant mineral in the human body.

name: Iron
description: Required for haemoglobin (the oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells). Deficiency causes anaemia. Haem iron from meat is more absorbable than non-haem iron from plants.

name: Magnesium
description: Involved in over 300 enzymatic reactions including energy production, protein synthesis, muscle and nerve function, and blood glucose control.

name: Phosphorus
description: Works with calcium to build bones and teeth. Also plays a role in energy metabolism (ATP) and cell membrane structure.

name: Potassium
description: Key electrolyte — an electrically charged mineral — that regulates fluid balance, nerve signals, and muscle contractions including the heart.

name: Sodium
description: Main electrolyte in extracellular fluid. Essential for nerve function and fluid balance. High intake is associated with elevated blood pressure.

name: Zinc
description: Supports immune function, wound healing, DNA synthesis, and taste/smell. Found richly in red meat, shellfish, and seeds.

name: Copper
description: Required for iron metabolism, connective tissue formation, and antioxidant enzymes. Found in shellfish, nuts, and organ meats.

name: Manganese
description: Activates enzymes involved in bone formation, metabolism, and antioxidant defence. Found in whole grains, nuts, and leafy greens.

name: Selenium
description: Antioxidant mineral that supports thyroid hormone metabolism and immune function. Found richly in Brazil nuts, seafood, and organ meats. Unit is micrograms (mcg = µg).

name: Iodine
description: Essential for thyroid hormone synthesis which regulates metabolism, growth, and development. Found in seafood, dairy, and iodised salt.

name: Chromium
description: Enhances insulin action and is involved in carbohydrate, fat, and protein metabolism. Found in broccoli, grapes, and whole grains.

name: Vitamin A
description: Fat-soluble vitamin critical for vision (especially night vision), immune function, and cell growth. Measured in mcg RAE (Retinol Activity Equivalents). Found as retinol in animal foods and provitamin A carotenoids in plants.

name: Vitamin C
description: Water-soluble antioxidant essential for collagen synthesis, immune function, and iron absorption. Not stored in the body — daily intake is needed.

name: Vitamin D
description: Fat-soluble vitamin synthesised by skin in sunlight. Essential for calcium absorption and bone health. Deficiency is very common. Measured in mcg (1 mcg = 40 IU).

name: Vitamin E
description: Fat-soluble antioxidant that protects cell membranes from oxidative damage. Found in nuts, seeds, and vegetable oils.

name: Vitamin K
description: Fat-soluble vitamin with two main forms: K1 (phylloquinone, from plants) for blood clotting and K2 (menaquinone, from fermented foods/animal products) for bone and cardiovascular health.

name: Thiamine
description: Water-soluble B vitamin essential for converting carbohydrates into energy and supporting nerve function. Found in whole grains, legumes, and pork.

name: Riboflavin
description: Water-soluble B vitamin involved in energy metabolism and cellular growth. Found in dairy, eggs, lean meats, and leafy greens.

name: Niacin
description: Water-soluble B vitamin critical for energy metabolism and DNA repair. Found in meat, fish, and whole grains. High doses can lower cholesterol.

name: Pantothenic Acid
description: Water-soluble B vitamin needed to synthesise Coenzyme A (CoA), which is central to fatty acid metabolism. Widely distributed in foods.

name: Vitamin B6
description: Water-soluble B vitamin involved in amino acid metabolism, neurotransmitter synthesis, and immune function.

name: Folate
description: Water-soluble B vitamin essential for DNA synthesis and cell division. Critical during pregnancy to prevent neural tube defects. Measured as mcg DFE (Dietary Folate Equivalents).

name: Vitamin B12
description: Water-soluble B vitamin essential for nerve function, red blood cell formation, and DNA synthesis. Found exclusively in animal products — vegans must supplement.

name: Choline
description: An essential nutrient (sometimes classified with B vitamins) critical for cell membrane structure, fat transport, and neurotransmitter acetylcholine synthesis. Found in eggs, liver, and soy.
